First, we look at two people who want to do something together. They make a plan about what each one will give or do. They say yes to this plan when they are together. But sometimes they stop working together. This happens because one person does not keep their word. Or maybe both of them go away with no clear time or place. The other person sees this and stops doing their part. The hard question is why some plans stay strong and others break. Some people think it is about trust. They believe that if you believe the other person will do what they said, you will safe work. If you do not trust someone, you might worry they will take your good ideas and run away. This fear makes people keep their word to be sure. Other people think it matters how the plan is set up. They see that both sides get something good from the plan. If the thing they get is better than the money or the help, they want to keep the plan. if the thing is worse or harder to get, they may let it go. Still others think it is just the way things are done. They look at how much time and effort is given before they plan to work together. If it was very easy for them to find friends, they might keep the plan going because they know what will happen if they leave would still need to work together later. In the end, promises hold together when both sides get what they want, when they trust each other, and when the time between them looks good.
